nodes.text: You open your eyes in a dim stone chapel. Rain hammers the roof. A voice echoes in your skull: 'Rise, Tarnished.' You don't remember your name. You don't remember anything. But your body remembers how to fight — your hands already ache for a weapon.

choices.label: Examine the glowing light on the floor

nodes.text: A golden thread of light spirals from the stone floor — a Site of Grace. When you touch it, warmth floods through you. Memories stir. You are Tarnished, called back by the Erdtree's fading light. The Elden Ring is shattered. Demigods hold its pieces. And you? You're the cleanup crew.

You find a rusted Longsword propped against the altar, a cracked Crimson Flask with healing liquid still inside, and a note: 'Seek the Church of Elleh. Merchant. Supplies. Don't die before you get there. — K'

You step through the chapel door and freeze. The Lands Between stretch before you — a vast, broken landscape under a fractured golden sky. The Erdtree towers in the distance, massive and wounded, its branches bleeding golden light. Below the cliff, a field of tombstones leads to open grassland. Something howls in the distance.

choices.label: Go back and grab the sword first

nodes.text: You drop down, hit the ground hard, and roll. Your ankles scream. But you're alive and moving. Ahead: tombstones, a winding path, and the faint shimmer of another grace.

choices.label: Follow the path through the graveyard

nodes.text: The Stranded Graveyard. Tombstones lean at drunk angles. Some of them are fresh. As you pass between them, a skeleton pulls itself from the dirt — empty eye sockets, rusted sword, and absolutely zero chill.

choices.label: Throw a rock to distract it

nodes.text: You swing the Longsword. The skeleton crumbles. Then reassembles. You smash it again. It reassembles again. This is going to be a long relationship. After the fourth time, it finally stays down. You learn an important lesson: in the Lands Between, even the dead don't know when to quit.

The rock clatters off a tombstone. The skeleton turns its empty skull to investigate. You slip past, heart hammering. It works — until you step on a branch. The skeleton's head swivels back toward you like a security camera.
You sprint through the graveyard, leaping over tombstones. More skeletons stir but you're faster. Barely. You burst out into open grassland, lungs burning, and collapse near a golden grace point.
Among the graves you find a Lantern — oil-fueled, still working. Useful for dark places. You also find a Golden Seed, pulsing with the Erdtree's power. It could strengthen your healing flask.

Limgrave opens before you like a painting that wants to kill you. Rolling green hills, ancient ruins, and — mounted on a spectral horse in the distance — a knight in golden armor. He hasn't seen you. The golden Erdtree looms behind everything, impossibly large.

choices.label: Head toward the Church of Elleh (the note mentioned it)
Follow the road east toward a lake

nodes.text: The grace steadies you. Golden light mends small wounds. You can see a ruined church to the north, and the road stretching east toward water. The Erdtree watches over everything like a disappointed parent.

choices.label: Head north to the Church of Elleh
Approach the golden knight on the road

nodes.text: You approach the golden knight. He's enormous — a Tree Sentinel, guardian of the Erdtree's paths. His horse snorts golden mist. He spots you and raises a halberd the size of a small tree. This was a mistake. This was absolutely a mistake.

You swing. He doesn't flinch. He swings back. You fly twenty feet and hit the ground so hard your ancestors feel it. The Tree Sentinel is a level 40 enemy and you are level nothing. This is the Lands Between's way of teaching humility.
The halberd comes down one final time. You see golden light. Then nothing. The Tree Sentinel returns to his patrol, unbothered. You were a speed bump on his afternoon commute.

end.title: You Died
end.reason: The Tree Sentinel is a lesson disguised as a boss fight. The lesson is: come back later.

nodes.text: You run. He doesn't chase — he's a guardian, not a hunter. But the message is clear: the Lands Between has things in it that will end you without even trying. You stumble toward the Church of Elleh, humbled and bruised.

The Church of Elleh is half-collapsed but still standing. Inside, firelight flickers. A merchant sits by the flames, humming a tune that sounds like it's been sad for centuries. He looks up. 'Ah. Another one the Erdtree spat back out.'

choices.label: Check the anvil near the wall

nodes.text: 'Name's Kale,' the merchant says, adjusting his hat. 'I sell things. You buy things. Simple economy for complicated times.' He has a Shield, a Smithing Stone, and information — if you're willing to listen. 'The Gatefront ahead is crawling with Godrick's soldiers. Stormveil Castle beyond that. You'll need to be stronger, or smarter. Preferably both.'
Kale hands you a wooden shield. It's not fancy, but it'll keep a sword out of your chest. 'Block, don't just swing,' he advises. 'The dead who swing without thinking stay dead.'
A Smithing Stone — rough, glowing faintly. With an anvil, you could reinforce your weapon. Kale nods approvingly. 'Sharper blade, shorter fights. Shorter fights, longer life.'

choices.label: Use the anvil to upgrade your weapon

nodes.text: 'The Gatefront is Godrick's forward camp. Soldiers everywhere — bored, violent, loyal to a madman who grafts other people's limbs onto himself. Charming fellow.' Kale leans closer. 'But there's a cellar under the ruins. Bypasses most of the camp. If you're sneaky enough.'

choices.label: Take the Shield before heading out

nodes.text: An old smithing anvil. Still functional. If you had a Smithing Stone, you could reinforce a weapon here.

You press the Smithing Stone against the blade. It dissolves into the metal with a hiss. The sword glows, settles — sharper, harder. Your Longsword is reinforced. It hums in your hand like it's grateful.

You rest at the grace. A figure materializes beside you — a young woman in a dark cloak. 'I am Melina,' she says. 'I offer you an accord. I will be your maiden — guide you to the Erdtree. In exchange, you carry me with you. I have... a purpose of my own.' Her eyes hold something old and burning.

choices.label: Ask her what her purpose is

nodes.text: 'Then it is done.' Melina fades, but her presence lingers — a warmth at the edge of your consciousness. You feel stronger. Guided. Less alone in this nightmare world. She whispers: 'Head north. Through the Gatefront. Stormveil Castle holds a shard of the Elden Ring. Godrick the Grafted guards it.'

choices.label: Explore the lake to the east first

nodes.text: Melina's expression doesn't change. 'Very well. The offer remains.' She fades. You're alone again. The Lands Between feels colder now. Self-reliance is admirable right up until it gets you killed.

choices.label: Explore the lake to the east

nodes.text: 'My purpose...' She pauses. Her visible eye flickers. 'I was burned once. By the world. By those who sought the Ring before you. I carry a purpose — same as you. That is enough for now.' She tilts her head. 'Do we have an accord?'

Agheel Lake stretches before you — shallow, still, scattered with drowned ruins. It's peaceful. Suspiciously peaceful. You've been in the Lands Between long enough to know that 'peaceful' just means something horrible hasn't noticed you yet.

choices.label: Wade into the ruins for treasure
Skirt the lake edge and head north

nodes.text: You wade through knee-deep water toward a flooded ruin. Stone stairs lead down. Inside, a chest — and the skeleton of whoever opened the last one. Encouraging.

choices.label: Leave it — that skeleton is a review

nodes.text: Inside: Spirit Ashes — a spectral summoning bell. With this, you can call spirit wolves in battle. The skeleton next to you seems supportive. In death, it's very encouraging.
You rest at the lakeside grace. The water laps gently. Then a massive shadow passes overhead. You look up. A dragon. An actual dragon, circling the lake. It doesn't see you. Yet.

choices.label: Move north quickly before it lands

nodes.text: Flying Dragon Agheel descends. The lake erupts in fire. Soldiers nearby become instant barbecue. The roar shakes the ground. You have seconds.

choices.label: Run north. Do NOT look back.

nodes.text: Dragonfire is not warm. It is the end of warmth. Agheel's flame washes over you and the last thing your brain registers is that this was entirely preventable.

end.title: You Died
end.reason: When a dragon is burning everything, the correct response is to run. You chose 'observe.'

nodes.text: The Gatefront Ruins rise ahead — crumbling walls, a large gate, and a camp of soldiers loyal to Godrick the Grafted. They haven't spotted you yet. The road through leads to Stormveil Castle. The road around leads to who knows where.
You watch from the hillside. Fifteen soldiers, a ballista on the wall, and near the back — a Site of Grace guarded by two sentries. There's also a cellar entrance half-hidden by rubble.

choices.label: Summon Spirit Ashes as a distraction

nodes.text: You draw the Longsword and charge. The first soldier barely turns before you cut him down. The alarm sounds. The whole camp is alive with shouting and steel.

choices.label: Disengage and flee into the ruins

nodes.text: You creep along the cliff edge above the camp. Below, soldiers patrol. One yawns. Another picks his nose. Godrick's finest. You slip past and reach a grace at the far side.
You raise the Spirit Ashes. Three spectral wolves materialize, snarling. They charge into camp. Soldiers panic, swinging at enemies they can barely see. Your path to the grace is clear.
Spectral wolves materialize beside you, howling. The soldiers falter. You press the advantage, cutting through confusion. By the time the wolves fade, you've reached the other side.
You fight through. Sword meets sword. You take hits — arm, side. But the Longsword sings and soldiers fall. By the end, you're standing in a circle of bodies, bleeding hard.

choices.label: Drink Crimson Flask and push to grace

nodes.text: You drop into the cellar. Darkness. Cobwebs. Old wine and something worse. You feel along the walls and find stairs leading up — emerging behind the main camp, near a grace.

choices.label: Climb the stairs to the grace

nodes.text: Your Lantern reveals a Rune Arc in the corner — a curved golden fragment humming with concentrated grace. Pocketed.
The Gatefront grace steadies you. From here, the path winds north up Stormhill toward Stormveil Castle. Dark clouds wrap its towers. Lightning flashes. Even the weather is dramatic in this place.

choices.label: Take the main road toward Stormveil
Look for a safer route around
Use the Rune Arc to prepare

nodes.text: You crush the Rune Arc. Golden energy floods your veins. Muscles tighten. Vision sharpens. For a moment you feel what it must be like to be a demigod — powerful, certain, untouchable. It won't last, but right now you're ready for anything.
The road climbs. Wind howls. Rain bites. You pass crucified bodies along the roadside — Godrick's welcome committee. Ahead, a crumbling shack and a hunched figure beside it. Soldier? Traveler? Trap?

Not a soldier — a wounded knight. No lord's banner. 'Tarnished,' he croaks. 'I am Rogier. I sought Stormveil... and Stormveil broke me.' He coughs blood. 'There's a side passage through the cliffs. Godrick doesn't guard it. Too arrogant.'

choices.label: Take his intel and move on

nodes.text: 'The side passage enters through the cliffs east of the main gate. Storm-hawk feathers mark the path.' He grips your arm. 'Godrick grafts the limbs of the fallen onto himself. Do not let him take yours.'

choices.label: Take the side passage to the castle

nodes.text: You hand Rogier the flask. Color returns to his face. He stares at you — kindest or most foolish person he's met. Possibly both. He tells you everything about Stormveil's defenses. Every patrol. Every weak point.

choices.label: Use his intel — take the side passage
Storm the main gate with his knowledge

nodes.text: You huddle in the shack. Rain drums the roof. A rat watches you with landlord confidence. You rest, but the wind carries sounds from the castle — screams, hammering, something that might be laughter but sounds like grinding bones.

choices.label: Move out when the rain eases

nodes.text: You crest a hill and there it is: Stormveil Castle. Massive. Ancient. Every window dark except those lit by fire. The main gate is ahead, heavily guarded. To the east, a narrow cliff path — unguarded.

You take the long way around, skirting the cliffs east of Stormveil. The detour leads through a misty ravine — Liurnia territory. The air changes. Colder. Wetter. But you're alive, and alive is the best stat you've got.

You approach Stormveil's main gate. A gatekeeper sneers: 'Another Tarnished. Godrick will add your arms to his collection.' The portcullis rises. You step inside. Whatever happens next, you survived the awakening.

end.title: Act 1 Complete: The Main Gate
end.reason: You entered Stormveil through the front door. Bold. Reckless. Exactly what a Tarnished would do.

nodes.text: You find the cliff passage — storm-hawk feathers marking the way. The path is narrow, the wind savage. But you make it, slipping into Stormveil unseen. Godrick doesn't know you're here. Yet.

end.title: Act 1 Complete: The Hidden Path
end.reason: You infiltrated Stormveil through the side passage. Cunning over courage.

nodes.text: You bypass Stormveil entirely, descending into misty Liurnia. The Academy of Raya Lucaria glows in the distance. You skipped a boss. Speedrunners everywhere salute you. But Godrick still holds his shard of the Ring.

end.title: Act 1 Complete: The Detour
end.reason: You avoided Stormveil and pushed into Liurnia. The ring shards can wait. Survival comes first.
